system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.96;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1004.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.8e-05;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='k', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      k;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            turbulentIntensityKineticEnergyInlet;
        intensity       0.05;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform 1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    innerWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outerWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='nut', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      nut;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    Default_Boundary_Region
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    inlet
    {
        type            nutkWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            nutkWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1000;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform 300;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            flowRateInletVelocity;
        massFlowRate    0.5;
        profile         turbulentBL;
        value           uniform (0 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            pressureInletOutletVelocity;
        value           uniform (0 0 0);
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 110000;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 100000;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='alphat', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      alphat;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='epsilon', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      epsilon;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -3 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 200;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            turbulentMixingLengthDissipationRateInlet;
        mixingLength    0.005;
        value           uniform 200;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform 200;
        value           uniform 200;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            epsilonWallFunction;
        value           uniform 200;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            epsilonWallFunction;
        value           uniform 200;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            epsilonWallFunction;
        value           uniform 200;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            epsilonWallFunction;
        value           uniform 200;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content="FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)
    // Back face at x = -50 (viewed from -x, should be CCW)
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 0
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 1
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 3

    // Block 1 - upper bend arm (x: 0 to 25, y: -25 to 25)
    // Back face at x = 0 (shared with Block 0 front face)
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 4
    (  25 -25  25 )   // 5
    (  25  25  25 )   // 6
    (   0  25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 2 - outlet transition (x: 25 to -100, y: -75 to -25)
    // Back face at x = 25 (shared with Block 1 front face)
    (  25 -75 -25 )   // 8
    ( -100 -75 -25 )  // 9
    ( -100 -25 -25 )  // 10
    (  25 -25 -25 )   // 11
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)
    // Back face (0,1,2,3) at x=-50, front face (4,5,6,7) at x=0
    // Viewed from +x: back face 0->1->2->3 should be CCW
    // 0(-50,-25,-25), 1(0,-25,-25), 2(0,25,-25), 3(-50,25,-25)
    // From +x view: 0(left,bottom) -> 1(right,bottom) -> 2(right,top) -> 3(left,top) = CCW ✓
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm (x: 0 to 25, y: -25 to 25)
    // Back face (4,5,6,7) at x=0, front face (8,9,10,11) at x=25
    // Viewed from +x: back face 4->5->6->7 should be CCW
    // 4(0,-25,25), 5(25,-25,25), 6(25,25,25), 7(0,25,25)
    // From +x view: 4(left,bottom) -> 5(right,bottom) -> 6(right,top) -> 7(left,top) = CCW ✓
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet transition (x: 25 to -100, y: -75 to -25)
    // Back face (8,9,10,11) at x=25, front face (9,10,15,14) at x=-100
    // But we need to share face 8,9,10,11 with Block 1's front face
    // Block 1 front face is 8,9,10,11. For Block 2, this shared face is the BACK face.
    // For Block 2 back face (8,9,10,11) viewed from +x (i.e., looking toward -x),
    // 8(25,-75,-25), 9(-100,-75,-25), 10(-100,-25,-25), 11(25,-25,-25)
    // From +x view: 8(right,bottom) -> 9(left,bottom) -> 10(left,top) -> 11(right,top)
    // This is CW! Need to reorder or accept that the shared face orientation must match.
    // Instead, let's make Block 2 use vertices 8,11,7,10 for the back face so it matches Block 1.
    // Actually, let me redefine Block 2 to go from x=25 to x=-100 with back face at x=25.
    // Block 1 front face is 8,9,10,11. Block 2 back face MUST be 8,9,10,11 in the order that makes it CCW when viewed from +x.
    // Currently: 8(25,-75,-25), 9(-100,-75,-25), 10(-100,-25,-25), 11(25,-25,-25)
    // From +x: 8(right,bottom), 9(left,bottom), 10(left,top), 11(right,top) = CW.
    // To get CCW: 8(right,bottom) -> 11(right,top) -> 10(left,top) -> 9(left,bottom)
    // So Block 2 back face is 8,11,10,9. But since this is the BACK face, the hex order is:
    // back_face back_vertices front_face front_vertices
    // hex (back0 back1 back2 back3 front0 front1 front2 front3)
    // With back_face = 8,11,10,9 and front_face = ? at x=-100
    // front vertices would be at x=-100: let's use 12,13,14,15
    // But to keep simple grading consistent, let me use the same y-extent as others.
    // Actually, let me re-read requirements. User said outlet points at (-500,...) but we'll use given verts.
    // Let me redefine with proper CCW ordering for ALL blocks.
    // Block 2: outlet transition (x: 25 to -100, y: -75 to -25)
    // Back face at x=25: vertices 8(25,-75,-25), 11(25,-25,-25), 7(0,-25,-25), 9(-100,-75,-25)
    // Wait, 9 is at x=-100. Let me use:
    // back_face: 8(25,-75,-25), 11(25,-25,-25), 7(0,-25,-25), 9(-100,-75,-25) - but 9 doesn't share y-range.
    // Simpler: use standard approach with proper CCW.
    // Let me restart with a clean 3-block approach where all shared faces match.
);

// Rewriting with guaranteed correct topology
scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 0
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 1
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 3
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 4
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 5
    (   0  25  25 )   // 6
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm (x: 0 to 25, y: -25 to 25)
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 8 (shared with Block 0 front bottom)
    (  25 -25  25 )   // 9
    (  25  25  25 )   // 10
    (   0  25  25 )   // 11 (shared with Block 0 front top)

    // Block 2: Outlet transition (x: 25 to -100, y: -75 to -25)
    (  25 -75 -25 )   // 12 (shared with Block 1 back bottom)
    ( -100 -75 -25 )  // 13
    ( -100 -25 -25 )  // 14
    (  25 -25 -25 )   // 15 (shared with Block 1 back top)
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Back face at x=-50: 0,1,2,3. Viewed from +x, should be CCW: 0(left,bottom), 1(right,bottom), 2(right,top), 3(left,top) = CCW ✓
    // Front face at x=0: 4,5,6,7. Viewed from -x (into domain), should be CCW when seen from outside (+x direction means looking toward -x)
    // Actually, for hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7):
    //   back_face  = 0,1,2,3 (at x=-50)
    //   front_face = 4,5,6,7 (at x=0)
    //   hex = back0,back1,back2,back3,front0,front1,front2,front3
    // Viewed from +x: back face 0->1->2->3 should appear CCW
    // 0(-50,-25,-25), 1(0,-25,-25), 2(0,25,-25), 3(-50,25,-25) = CCW ✓
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm
    // Back face at x=0: 4,5,6,7. Viewed from +x, should be CCW: 4(left,bottom), 5(right,bottom), 6(right,top), 7(left,top) = CCW ✓
    // Front face at x=25: 8,9,10,11
    // 8(0,-25,25), 9(25,-25,25), 10(25,25,25), 11(0,25,25)
    // From +x: 8(left,bottom), 9(right,bottom), 10(right,top), 11(left,top) = CCW ✓
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet transition
    // Back face at x=25: 8,9,10,11. Viewed from +x, should be CCW: 8(left,bottom), 9(right,bottom), 10(right,top), 11(left,top) = CCW ✓
    // Front face at x=-100: 12,13,14,15
    // 12(25,-75,-25), 13(-100,-75,-25), 14(-100,-25,-25), 15(25,-25,-25)
    // From +x: 12(right,bottom), 13(left,bottom), 14(left,top), 15(right,top) = CW ✗
    // Need CCW: 12(right,bottom) -> 15(right,top) -> 14(left,top) -> 13(left,bottom)
    // So back_face = 12,15,14,13
    // front_face = ? At x=-100, we need vertices that correspond
    // Let me use 13,14,15,12 as the front face but the hex order is:
    // hex (back0 back1 back2 back3 front0 front1 front2 front3)
    // With back_face = 12,15,14,13 and front_face = ?, but we want front vertices at x=-100
    // The front face vertices are at x=-100: 13,14,?,?. We have 13(-100,-75,-25), 14(-100,-25,-25)
    // Need two more at x=-100 with z=25. Let me add them.
    // Actually, let me reuse existing vertices properly.
    // The front face should be at x=-100: let's use 13,14,16,17
    // But to avoid adding vertices, notice that for the hex to work, the front face
    // vertices are at x=-100 and complete the shape.
    // Since we're constrained by the vertex list, let me define:
    // front vertices at x=-100: 13(-100,-75,-25), 14(-100,-25,-25), 16(-100,-25,25), 17(-100,-75,25)
    // But that adds vertices. Let me instead use the standard hex ordering trick.
    // For a valid hex, I need 8 distinct vertices. Let me redefine Block 2 properly.
);

// Final correct version
scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x: -50 to 0, y: -25 to 25)
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 0
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 1
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 3
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 4
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 5
    (   0  25  25 )   // 6
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm (x: 0 to 25, y: -25 to 25)
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 8 (same as Block 0 front bottom)
    (  25 -25  25 )   // 9
    (  25  25  25 )   // 10
    (   0  25  25 )   // 11 (same as Block 0 front top)

    // Block 2: Outlet transition (x: 25 to -100, y: -75 to -25)
    (  25 -75 -25 )   // 12 (same as Block 1 back bottom)
    ( -100 -75 -25 )  // 13
    ( -100 -25 -25 )  // 14
    (  25 -25 -25 )   // 15 (same as Block 1 back top)
    ( -100 -75  25 )  // 16
    ( -100 -25  25 )  // 17
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Vertices 0,1,2,3 at x=-50; 4,5,6,7 at x=0
    // Back face (0,1,2,3) viewed from +x: CCW = 0(lft,btt),1(rgt,btt),2(rgt,tpp),3(lft,tpp) ✓
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm
    // Vertices 4,5,6,7 at x=0; 8,9,10,11 at x=25
    // Back face (4,5,6,7) viewed from +x: CCW = 4(lft,btt),5(rgt,btt),6(rgt,tpp),7(lft,tpp) ✓
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet transition
    // Vertices 12,13,14,15 at x=25; 13,14,17,16 at x=-100
    // But 13,14 are shared. For Block 2, back face is at x=25: vertices 12,15,14,13
    // back_face = 12,15,14,13. Viewed from +x: 12(rgt,btt),15(rgt,tpp),14(lft,tpp),13(lft,btt) = CCW ✓
    // front_face at x=-100: 16,17,?,? - but we need to map properly
    // The hex order is: hex (back0 back1 back2 back3 front0 front1 front2 front3)
    // So hex (12 15 14 13 16 17 11 10)
    // Check: back=(12,15,14,13), front=(16,17,11,10)
    // 12(25,-75,-25), 15(25,-25,-25), 14(-100,-25,-25), 13(-100,-75,-25)
    // 16(-100,-75,25), 17(-100,-25,25), 11(0,25,25), 10(25,25,25)
    // This mixes blocks... let me use:
    // Standard hex format: hex(v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)
    // where (v0-v3) is one face, (v4-v7) is opposite face,
    // and v_i connects to v_{i+4 mod 4}
    // For Block 2, we want:
    //   back face at x=25: 12,15,14,13 (CCW when viewed from +x)

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) ... has inward-pointing faces` — Block 1 has faces with inward-pointing normals.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — cascading from blockMesh failure.

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the block topology has been refined across many attempts. The current vertices and blocks are:

**Vertices:**
- 0(-50,-25,-25), 1(0,-25,-25), 2(0,25,-25), 3(-50,25,-25)
- 4(-50,-25,25), 5(0,-25,25), 6(0,25,25), 7(-50,25,25)
- 8(0,-25,25), 9(25,-25,25), 10(25,25,25), 11(0,25,25)
- 12(25,-75,-25), 13(-100,-75,-25), 14(-100,-25,-25), 15(25,-25,-25), 16(-100,-75,25), 17(-100,-25,25)

**Blocks:**
- Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — inlet straight
- Block 1: `hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6)` — bend arm (this is the problematic one)
- Block 2: `hex (12 15 14 13 16 17 11 10)` — outlet transition

The error on Block 1 (`hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6)`) states it has **inward-pointing faces**. The face `(4 8 11 7)` (normal calculation: (4→8)×(4→11) = (25,0,0)×(75,0,0) = z-direction) and `(7 10 15 11)` give clues that the block geometry creates non-convexity.

## Key Insight: The bend geometry needs proper block separation

The fundamental issue is that the bend region where the flow turns from +x to -y direction requires careful block partitioning. The current attempt merges the bend and transition into one block with complex shared edges.

## Proposed Solution: Use Exactly 3 Well-Formed Blocks

**Redefine the blocks with verified convex geometry:**

1. **Block 0 (Inlet Straight, x=-50 to x=0, y=-25 to y=25):**  
   `hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)` — verified CCW from outside on both faces

2. **Block 1 (Upper Bend Arm, x=0 to x=25, y=-25 to y=0):**  
   `hex (7 11 10 6 3 15 14 2)` — but verify the shared face with Block 0 is vertices (2,3,7,6). For Block 0 `(3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)`, the face is 2→1? Actually, with Block 0 as `(3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)`, the face at y=25 connects vertices 2,3,7,6. For Block 1, this shared face must be 2→3→15→14. The full Block 1 becomes `hex (7 11 10 6 2 15 14 3)` — but this isn't a proper hex. **Simpler approach: remove Block 1a entirely and make Block 1 span the full y-range from y=-25 to y=25 over x=0 to x=25.** This eliminates the complex multi-face junction.

3. **Block 2 (Outlet Transition, x=25 to x=-100, y=-75 to y=-25):**  
   `hex (12 15 14 13 16 17 11 10)` — but verify connectivity. Better yet, make this a simpler block spanning x=25 to x=-100 with full y-range from -75 to -25, connected properly to Block 1 at x=25.

**The critical fix:** Every boundary face must be individually verified and corrected. For each face in `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, ensure that when viewed from outside the domain, the vertices wind **counter-clockwise**. This is the golden rule — don't inherit ordering, verify each one.

## Error 2: Cascading `rhoSimpleFoam` error

This will resolve automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.</reviewer_analysis>
